In [33]:
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')

# Force override the backend before gwpy loads
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.switch_backend('Agg')

import gwpy
from gwpy.timeseries import TimeSeries

Hanford - observatory in Washington, Hanford Livingston - observatory in Livingston, Lousiana

  • Data within this timeframe contains an observation of gravitational waves from a blackhole merger

paper of observation

  • https://journals.aps.org/prl/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevLett.116.061102 notebook inspiration
  • https://gwosc.org/GW150914data/LOSC_Event_tutorial_GW150914.html
In [34]:
livingston = TimeSeries.fetch_open_data('L1', 1126259446, 1126259478) # time of first measured gravitational waves
print('livingston done loading')

livingston done loading
In [35]:
hanford = TimeSeries.fetch_open_data('H1', 1126259446, 1126259478) # # time of first measured gravitational waves
print("hanford done loading")

hanford done loading
In [36]:
hanford.plot()
Out[36]:
In [37]:
livingston.plot()
Out[37]:
In [38]:
specgram = livingston.spectrogram(20, fftlength=8, overlap=4) ** (1/2.)

Pre-processed spectrogram of Livinston data¶

In [39]:
plot = specgram.plot(norm='log', vmin=5e-24, vmax=1e-20)
ax = plot.gca()
ax.set_ylim(10, 2000)
ax.set_yscale('log')
ax.colorbar(label=r'GW strain ASD [strain/$\sqrt{\mathrm{Hz}}$]')
plot.savefig('raw_livingston_spectrogram.png')
In [40]:
from IPython.display import Image
Image('raw_livingston_spectrogram.png')
Out[40]:

Filtering livingston and hanford data with a bandpass for Timeseries plot¶

In [41]:
start_time = 1126259462.4 - 2
end_time = 1126259462.4 + 2
filtered_livingston = livingston.whiten(4,2).bandpass(35, 350).crop(start_time, end_time)
filtered_hanford = hanford.whiten(4,2).bandpass(35, 350).crop(start_time, end_time)
In [42]:
plot = filtered_livingston.plot()
plot.save('filtered_livingston.png')
In [43]:
Image('filtered_livingston.png')
Out[43]:
In [44]:
plot = filtered_hanford.plot()
plot.save('filtered_hanford.png')
In [45]:
Image('filtered_hanford.png')
Out[45]:

Filtering Spectrogram Data¶

In [46]:
import numpy as np
import scipy
In [47]:
whitened_livingston = livingston.whiten(4, 2).crop(1126259457, 1126259465)
In [48]:
# Livingston
strain_whitened_l = whitened_livingston.value
fs = int(whitened_livingston.sample_rate.value)
times_l = whitened_livingston.times.value

indxt_l = np.where((times_l >= tevent - deltat) & (times_l < tevent + deltat))[0]

NFFT = int(fs / 16)
NOVL = int(NFFT * 15 / 16)
window = np.blackman(NFFT)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.specgram(strain_whitened_l[indxt_l], NFFT=NFFT, Fs=fs, window=window,
             noverlap=NOVL, cmap='ocean', xextent=[-deltat, deltat])
plt.xlabel('Time (s) since ' + str(tevent))
plt.ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')
plt.colorbar()
plt.axis([-deltat, deltat, 0, 500])
plt.title('LIGO Livingston whitened spectrogram near GW150914')
plt.savefig('livingston_chirp.png')
Image('livingston_chirp.png')
Out[48]:
In [49]:
whitened_hanford = hanford.whiten(4, 2).crop(1126259457, 1126259465)
In [50]:
# Hanford
strain_whitened_h = whitened_hanford.value
fs = int(whitened_hanford.sample_rate.value)
times_h = whitened_hanford.times.value

indxt_h = np.where((times_h >= tevent - deltat) & (times_h < tevent + deltat))[0]

NFFT = int(fs / 16)
NOVL = int(NFFT * 15 / 16)
window = np.blackman(NFFT)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.specgram(strain_whitened_h[indxt_h], NFFT=NFFT, Fs=fs, window=window,
             noverlap=NOVL, cmap='ocean', xextent=[-deltat, deltat])
plt.xlabel('Time (s) since ' + str(tevent))
plt.ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')
plt.colorbar()
plt.axis([-deltat, deltat, 0, 500])
plt.title('LIGO Hanford whitened spectrogram near GW150914')
plt.savefig('hanford_chirp.png')
Image('hanford_chirp.png')
